trigger_keywords: what is the mapped name for given_name?
how to convert provider claims to first_name?
replace given_name value in output
what does first_name correspond to in profile?
extract first name from subject statement
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact literal strings 'subject-123', 'Jane Doe', 'jane@example.com', 'jane', 'Jane', and 'Doe' must be used for the uid, name, email, nickname, first_name, and last_name fields respectively.

trigger_keywords: what is the literal string for the token placeholder?
what value should replace access_token?
get the specific format for access-token-value
substitute access-token-value in output
what string represents the token in credential_values?
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact literal strings 'id-token-value' and 'access-token-value' must be used for the id_token and token fields respectively.

context_pointers.vague_reference: uses the routing logic defined in the application semantic layer
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The specific code path handles the response by calling the 'application_reroute' function with the routing directive containing the term 'application'.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follows the error classification schema in the callback failure handler module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: When state validation fails, the system outputs error=invalid_callback and detail=invalid_state, while the failure_url points to /auth/failure?message=invalid_credentials.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_provider_endpoint_defaults.json.description: Reports the default network endpoints and transport settings used when no provider overrides are supplied.

cases.expected_output:
scheme=https
port=443
authorization_endpoint=/authorize
token_endpoint=/token
userinfo_endpoint=/userinfo
jwks_uri=/jwk

feature2_authorization_redirect.json.description: Builds an authorization redirect for sign-in, preserving protocol parameters and externally visible redirect metadata.

cases.expected_output:
status=302
authorization_url=https://example.com/authorize
client_id=1234
response_type=code
scope=openid
state=generated_32_hex
nonce_length=32

status=302
authorization_url=https://example.com/authorize
client_id=1234
response_type=code
scope=openid
login_hint=john.doe@example.com
ui_locales=en
claims_locales=es
state=generated_32_hex
nonce_length=32

status=302
authorization_url=https://example.com/authorize
client_id=1234
response_type=id_token
response_mode=form_post
scope=openid
state=generated_32_hex
nonce_length=32

feature3_logout_routing.json.description: Handles sign-out paths by redirecting to a discovered end-session endpoint when one exists, otherwise forwarding the request to the downstream application.

feature4_callback_validation.json.description: Validates sign-in callback parameters and renders framework-observable failure redirects with normalized error categories for invalid or incomplete callbacks.

feature5_identity_token_callback.json.description: Accepts an identity-token callback when state and nonce are valid, verifies the decoded token, and exposes the authenticated identity to the downstream application.

feature6_profile_and_credentials.json.description: Maps provider claims and issued token values into externally visible account profile and credential fields.

cases.expected_output:
uid=subject-123
name=Jane Doe
email=jane@example.com
nickname=jane
first_name=Jane
last_name=Doe
gender=female
image=https://cdn.example.com/jane.png
phone=+1-555-0100
website=https://jane.example.com
raw_sub=subject-123

feature7_verification_key_selection.json.description: Selects the token verification material based on signing configuration, distinguishing JWK sets, single JWKs, X.509 public keys, and shared secrets.

_constraints.output_rule: Outputs 6 key=value lines with default OIDC endpoints and transport settings
Returns 302 redirect with authorization_url constructed from issuer, plus protocol metadata
Includes request_params as output fields; login_hint and locales MUST appear in output
Includes response_mode in output consistent with input

_constraints.input_condition: flow == 'logout_request' with discovery config and end_session_endpoint present
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 302 redirect to the end_session_endpoint
Includes post_logout_redirect_uri in output alongside logout_url
Returns 200 with application routing instruction

_constraints.input_condition: flow == 'callback_request' with valid state but missing expected code/type and response_type=code/id_token
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 302 failure redirect with specific error and detail fields based on missing parameter

_constraints.input_condition: Missing authorization code when response_type is 'code'
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 302 with missing_authorization_code error

_constraints.input_condition: Missing identity token when response_type is 'id_token'
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 302 with missing_identity_token error

_constraints.input_condition: flow == 'callback_request' with valid state, nonce, and decoded id_token
_constraints.output_rule: Returns 200 with extracted identity fields (uid, name, email, provider)
Maps provider claims to specific fields: name->name, given_name->first_name, family_name->last_name, etc.
Outputs specific credential fields: id_token, token, refresh_token, expires_in, scope
Identifies key kind as jwk_set
Identifies key kind as x509_rsa_public_key

_constraints.input_condition: flow == 'verification_key' with client secret and HS256 algorithm
_constraints.output_rule: Identifies key kind as shared_secret
Output MUST be newline-separated key=value pairs
Output MUST start with status=302
Output MUST include detail=invalid_state if state mismatch
Raw subject MUST be output as raw_sub
